quiz_items.question: Under which Act and chapter are laser product regulations established?
options: Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA), Chapter III
Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA), Chapter V, Subchapter C
Radiation Control for Health and Safety Act, Chapter II
Occupational Safety and Health Act, Section 5
quiz_items.explanation: Laser product regulations are established under the Federal Food, Drug and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA), Chapter V, Subchapter C - Electronic Product Radiation Control.

quiz_items.question: According to 21 CFR 1040.10(b)(19), a laser produces electromagnetic radiation in the wavelength range from ______ nm to ______ nm.
quiz_items.explanation: The regulation defines a laser as a device capable of producing electromagnetic radiation in the wavelength range from 180 to 1 x 10^6 nanometers (1 million nm) by controlled stimulated emission.

quiz_items.question: A company modifies existing laser products by adding new control software. Under 21 CFR 1040.10(i), is this company considered a manufacturer?
options: Yes, because they are modifying laser products
No, because they are only changing software
Only if the modification affects laser output power
Only if they also manufacture the original hardware
quiz_items.explanation: Under 21 CFR 1040.10(i), a manufacturer includes any person or organization modifying laser products. Software modifications that affect laser operation would qualify the company as a manufacturer with associated regulatory responsibilities.
